post.title: ✨ Celebrating years of dedication at DOGA ✨
post.content:
Last week, we came together to honor the commitment and career journeys of our colleagues who have been part of the DOGA team for 10, 20, and 25 years.
It was a meaningful event where the traditional service pins were awarded to our colleagues who reached these important milestones — a tribute made even more special by the presence of loved ones and the warm support of coworkers.
The celebration continued with an outdoor gathering in the restaurant gardens, where laughter, stories, and great food created the perfect atmosphere to reflect on the past and look forward to the future.
To everyone who made this event possible: thank you. Let’s keep building the future together!

It was a meaningful event where the traditional service pins were awarded to our colleagues who reached these important milestones — a tribute made even more special by the presence of loved ones and the warm support of coworkers. The celebration continued with an outdoor gathering in the restaurant gardens, where laughter, stories, and great food created the perfect atmosphere to reflect on the past and look forward to the future. post.title: Light Mobility Cluster and CIAC welcomed to DOGA
post.content:
On June 10, DOGA welcomed members of the Light Mobility Cluster and CIAC - Clúster de la Indústria d'Automoció de Catalunya for an #intercluster conference focused on #mobility: "From Motorcycles to Automotive – A Path to Industrial Competitiveness".
The event explored the main #challenges and emerging #opportunities in the components industry, highlighting the sector’s current #transformation.
Organized by both clusters, the conference featured insightful presentations from Eurecat - Centro Tecnológico and SGS, followed by a roundtable discussion with SHAD, Meleghy Automotive, and DOGA.
post.excerpt: On June 10, DOGA welcomed members of the Light Mobility Cluster and CIAC – Clúster de la Indústria d’Automoció de Catalunya for an #intercluster conference focused on #mobility: “From Motorcycles to Automotive – A Path to Industrial Competitiveness”. The event explored the main #challenges and emerging #opportunities in the components industry, highlighting the sector’s current #transformation. Organized by both clusters, the conference featured insightful presentations from Eurecat – Centro Tecnológico and SGS, followed by a roundtable discussion with SHAD, Meleghy Automotive, and DOGA.

post.title: EUROBIKE has come to an end
post.content: We is proud of the strong interest and positive response to our new e-bike motor, D-GO Mobility. Developed by our #Electromobility Division, D-GO represents a significant step forward in our commitment to #smarter mobility #solutions and a greener future.
post.excerpt: We is proud of the strong interest and positive response to our new e-bike motor, D-GO Mobility. Developed by our #Electromobility Division, D-GO represents a significant step forward in our commitment to #smarter mobility #solutions and a greener future.

post.title: 🏁 DOGA GRAND PRIX
post.content:
A Day of Speed, Team Spirit, and Fun!
Last week, our Technical Center turned into a racing arena for the DOGA GRAND PRIX — a unique internal event where employees competed on professional racing simulators, shared laughs, and enjoyed great food and music.
From thrilling final races to a generational showdown on the track, the day was filled with energy and connection. Events like this strengthen our culture and remind us how powerful teamwork can be — on and off the track.
Thanks to everyone who made it possible. See you at the next lap! 🏎️🎉
post.excerpt: A Day of Speed, Team Spirit, and Fun! Last week, our Technical Center turned into a racing arena for the DOGA GRAND PRIX — a unique internal event where employees competed on professional racing simulators, shared laughs, and enjoyed great food and music. From thrilling final races to a generational showdown on the track, the day was filled with energy and connection. Events like this strengthen our culture and remind us how powerful teamwork can be — on and off the track. Thanks to everyone who made it possible. See you at the next lap! 🏎️🎉

post.title: Washer Tanks for Agricultural machinery
post.content:
At DOGA, we are specialists in the design and manufacturing of 💧 Windshield Washer Tanks 💧 for agricultural machinery such as #tractors and #harvesters. Produced through high-precision blow molding, our plastic tanks — ranging from 1 to 40 liters, #standard or fully #customized — ensure durable and reliable performance under demanding conditions. Agricultural machinery requires robust and efficient components: our washer #tanks are a guarantee of optimal operation, ensuring effective cleaning of vision systems and supporting the overall performance of the equipment. Combined with our sealed #pumps (IP67) and customizable connectors, we offer a complete and secure #solution for every application.
🚜 Work better. See clearer. Enjoy the colours of the field.

post.title: 🌿 Eco-design in Action! 🌿
post.excerpt: 1st week of July, we finished a motivating Eco-design Workshop in collaboration with the specialized environmental consultancy inèdit.   Throughout two sessions, the cross-functional team from DOGA reviewed the forth-coming and applicable legislations regarding Eco-design and their impact on DOGA products. They also had the opportunity to analyse the DOGA Product Life Cycle, identifying the most critical issues.   This training initiative is designed to integrate environmental criteria into every stage of product development, promoting a more conscious design culture aligned with today’s industrial challenges.   At DOGA, we continue moving forward toward a more sustainable, innovative, and responsible company!🌍🌱 […]

post.title: Code of Conduct
post.content: DOGA GROUP CODE OF CONDUCT Introduction The companies which are part of DOGA (hereinafter referred to as DOGA) have, and have had since its foundation in 1958, an excellent reputation all over the world for corporate honesty. Integrity as a basis for business management, compliance with rules and laws, as well as our corporate values and commitment to sustainable development have been, are and will be the guidelines to be followed in decision-making processes and action taking. Scope of the Code of Conduct DOGA’s Code of Conduct has been laid down to formulate the principles by which the Group establishes its relations with employees, suppliers, partners and other participating parties. It is applicable to all the members of the organisation, from the Board of Directors to every single employee. DOGA encourages its suppliers, customers and other related parties to adhere to these principles. The principles in this document will be applied to the evaluation of present and potential suppliers. Business Principles Compliance with current legislation DOGA complies with the local rules and legislation in all the countries where it is present. In situations in which there is no specific legislation, DOGA applies its own rules based on its corporate values and corporate culture. If the law and the principles established in this code come into conflict, the law will prevail. Relations with customers and suppliers The transactions between DOGA and its customers and suppliers are characterized by fairness. DOGA will on no account offer any rewards or any advantage to those customers, potential customers or any of its representatives for breaking the applicable law or challenging the reasonable, ethical and generally accepted business practices. DOGA employees should under no circumstances accept payments, presents or any other type of reward by a third party which could affect or appear to affect their objectivity in their decision making. Financial information All DOGA financial transactions must be reported according to the generally accepted accounting practices. The accounting records must show the nature of all transactions correctly and should never lead to error or deceit. DOGA will provide open, truthful, relevant and comprehensible information on time to those who require such information, if DOGA is under obligation to do so. Conflicts of interest DOGA employees and the members of the Board of Directors will develop their private and other external activities, such as their own financial interests, in a way in which they do not come into conflict, or seem to do so, with the interests of DOGA. Should any conflict of interest arise, it must be immediately reported to the Human Resources department by the person subject to the conflict. Political involvement DOGA observes neutrality with respect to political parties. Neither DOGA names nor DOGA assets should ever be used to promote the interests of political parties or any of their candidates. Environmental Principles Resources efficacy DOGA designs its products and processes in such a way that the energy and raw materials are used efficiently and the waste and residual products are reduced to the minimum during the life cycle of products. Preventive Principle DOGA supports the preventive principle by avoiding materials and processes which could be environmentally risky and could put health at risk whenever alternative procedures are available. Environmental performance DOGA audits periodically its own plants and its suppliers’ plants, keeps a record of and reports their environmental action, emphasising the evaluation of potential risks for the products. Human Rights and Work Practices Human Rights Within its scope of influence, DOGA supports and respects the protection of the human rights internationally proclaimed. Non discrimination DOGA employs and deals with its employees, customers, suppliers and other participating parties following a policy of no gender, race, religion, age, disability, sexual orientation, nationality, political opinion, affiliations, social or ethnic origin discrimination. Diversity is promoted at all levels. Labour No form of hard labour, child or compulsory labour will be tolerated. No one will be employed unless they have reached the age required to finish the compulsory secondary school. Freedom of association, the right to collective negotiation and the right to agreements will be protected in all DOGA workplaces. Working atmosphere All DOGA employees will be guaranteed the optimal conditions for a healthy and safe working atmosphere. The responsibilities of Managers and Employees It is DOGA Managers’ responsibility to communicate and inform about both the content and the spirit of this document within the organisation, and encourage the employees to report any behaviour which violates the principles established in this document. The explicit or implicit acceptance of questionable actions will not be tolerated. Reports on no compliance with this code can be sent anonymously and confidentially to DOGA General Management. A communication channel is established through the e-mail address dpd@doga.es, which is received by the HR Director and the data protection delegate. Those who communicate violations in good faith, will not be subjected to punishment. The application of this code will be accurate and coherent. The no compliance with its statements can lead to disciplinary actions. Adherence to the United Nations Global Compact DOGA joined the United Nations Global Compact on March 10th, 2015, therefore voluntarily acquiring a commitment to comply with the 10 Principles that are established in said Global Compact and taking up the obligation to prepare and share annual progress reports through the Global Compact web platform (www.pactomundial.org). The Code of Conduct has been adopted by the Board of Directors of DOGA on June 27, 2007; first revision on March 24, 2015 on the occasion of the company's adherence to the UN Global Compact; second revision on April 1, 2019 on the occasion of the incorporation of the communication channel with Management; third revision on March 1, 2023 on the occasion of the acquisition of the DOGA companies by the new owners. Any change will be published promptly.

post.title: Sales Conditions
post.content: DOGA S.A. - Autovía A-2, km. 583 - 08630 ABRERA - BARCELONA (SPAIN) · Tel. +34 93 770 46 00 · e-mail: doga@doga.es · www.doga.esGeneral Conditions of Sale – DOGA GROUPThe following General Conditions of Sale apply to DOGA GROUP, which includes the following subsidiaries: DOGA, SA - DOGA GESTIÓ, SLU – DOGA PARTS, SL. - DOGA Italia, S.R.L. - DOGA do Brasil, Ltda. - DOGA MEXICO COMPONENTES, SA DE C.V. - DOGA USA, Corp. - DOGA India, Pvt Ltd - DOGA NanTong Auto Parts Co., Ltd.1. Services ProvidedThe products/services will be supplied according to what has been established in the corresponding offer.When the offer is accepted, the client shall send an order to the DOGA GROUP subsidiary (“the subsidiary”), including the client’s order reference (if required), the offer reference, and the accepted points.2. OrdersThe contractual relationship starts when the client signs the first order; all orders are subject to acceptance by the subsidiary.All orders must be confirmed within 10 working days upon receipt. Claims cannot be made against unconfirmed orders.Dates and delivery times in the offer are indicative; actual delivery depends on supply times, manufacturing, and receipt of the order, and will be confirmed upon receipt.DOGA GROUP may unilaterally cancel manufacturing and/or delivery if insolvency arises or if no insurance has been taken out with the client or its subcontractors to cover business risks.3. Prices and InvoicesApplicable taxes are excluded from the offer; they will be included in the invoice.For regularly contracted services, the subsidiary may change prices for the following period.Advance payments are accepted up to 6 months before service. No price variation during this period, except 3.J). If canceled, the portion not supplied will be returned, minus 15% (admin/planning) and 100% of incurred material costs (including molds, if any).Acceptance implies payment under the offer’s specific terms, unaffected by any financial assistance or benefits.Prices may be reviewed if costs rise by more than 3%.Prices are exclusive and valid only for the company and location specified. Deliveries to other entities/locations may change prices.Prices are valid for the quantities in the offer. New quantities may change prices, or orders without quantity info may be rejected.No prompt payment discounts unless specifically stated in the contract.Deliveries and new orders will be automatically rejected if there are payment delays.Prices are set in the production site’s currency. If quoted in another currency, they are set to the exchange rate on the estimate day; if the rate varies by more than 5%, prices may be reviewed.Invoices are payable within 30 days of issue.Late payment accrues interest from the due date and collection expenses. If no rate agreed, interest is 8 percentage points above the ECB main refinancing rate.4. Claims / RefundsNo claims for losses caused by delivery date failures or quality issues unless specifically agreed.Quality-related claims will be managed under DOGA GROUP’s quality procedures.No refunds/payment requests without a positive resolution by DOGA’s Product Quality Department.A product is approved if accompanied by a client-completed Sample Acceptance Sheet or if the client sends a formal purchase order (not a prototype).DOGA GROUP is not liable for stoppages in the client’s production line.No quality claims accepted more than 60 days after client receipt.No returns accepted without an RMA issued by DOGA’s Product Quality Department.No returns/replacements until defective products are analyzed and DOGA GROUP responsibility is confirmed.If repair can be done by the client, DOGA GROUP may accept costs if informed in advance of labor rates and required hours.If DOGA GROUP opposes client repair, it may send its own staff; no unapproved costs will be borne.If an unauthorized product requires reprocessing at the client’s premises, DOGA GROUP will not bear costs unless specifically authorized or if presentation of goods was refused.No consequential losses accepted (e.g., reprocessing costs by the client).No design claims for products/projects not validated by the client. Sample acceptance implies approval and supply commitment. Claims for defects detected in accepted validation samples are not accepted unless a conditional acceptance (limited in time/quantity) was issued.Product liability claims are subject to insurer acceptance; DOGA GROUP’s liability is limited to the insurer’s agreement.5. CancellationsThe client may cancel only by written notice within 60 calendar days of the order’s end date (see 4.C).The subsidiary may cancel by written notice within 90 calendar days before execution. Notice is not required if the client fails to meet payment or other agreed obligations.6. LiabilityDOGA GROUP is not liable for failures or delays due to force majeure or circumstances beyond reasonable control.Only the client may take action; no liability for emerging damage or loss of profit, regardless of origin.7. Responsibilities of the ClientThe client will provide all information and documentation required to carry out the contracted services/products.The client agrees not to hire subsidiary employees during the contract and up to 6 months after termination. Breach entails compensation equal to one year’s salary of the hired worker at the time of hiring.8. Delivery. Passing of Risk. Retention of OwnershipAny agreed term is construed under the INCOTERMS in force upon signing. If none agreed, delivery is Free Carrier (FCA) at the destination specified by the subsidiary.For FCA, at the client’s request, the subsidiary will send the product to destination; risk passes when handed to the first carrier.Products remain the subsidiary’s property until fully paid.At the subsidiary’s request, the client will assist with measures to protect ownership.9. MiscellaneousIf the client’s general purchase conditions are not compatible, the client may cancel the order. Not canceling implies acceptance of these general conditions.Any differing conditions must be agreed in an agreement/supply contract. If none exists, these conditions prevail.No party may transfer this offer without the other party’s prior written consent.All drawings/technical documents submitted remain the property of the supplying party and may not be used or disclosed beyond their intended purpose without consent.Parties submit to the courts of the city where the subsidiary is registered; Spanish law applies.10. TransportationTransport costs for replacement/return of defective materials are borne by DOGA GROUP, provided they are managed by it and its suppliers.Excess transportation costs due to production line stoppages are borne by the client, provided the delivery date was duly confirmed by DOGA GROUP.11. Personal Data ProtectionIn compliance with the Spanish Personal Data Protection Law (Organic Law 15/1999), personal data provided will be incorporated into our files solely to manage the business relationship.The client may exercise rights of access, rectification, deletion, and objection by contacting dpd@doga.es.

post.title: Drive Systems Electronics
post.content:
ELECTRONICS
Discover Drive Systems Electronics
The Power to Move, the Power to Control Reliable, smooth, and precise performance in any system. Electronic Motors for Drive System Applications Smart Drive Systems for Ultimate Precision Experience unmatched control and efficiency with electronic motors powered by cutting-edge communication and regulation technologies. The Power to Move, the Power to Control Smooth and accurate operation for enhanced visibility Smart Drive Systems for Ultimate Precision Experience unmatched control and efficiency with electronic motors powered by cutting-edge communication and regulation technologies. Features and Functionalities
Electronic Motor Control
Seamless integration of CANOpen protocols (CiA 301 and CiA 402) to ensure standardized, reliable communication across industrial networks.
Control Modes
Speed Mode: Allows for highly precise adjustment of motor speed.Position Mode: Controls motor movement to reach a specific position.Torque Mode: Regulates the rotational force applied by the motor, ideal for applications requiring torque control.
Parameter Configuration
Allows adjustment of various control parameters to optimize performance according to the specific needs of each application.
Seamless integration of CANOpen protocols (CiA 301 and CiA 402) to ensure standardized, reliable communication across industrial networks.Speed Mode: Allows for highly precise adjustment of motor speed.Position Mode: Controls motor movement to reach a specific position.Torque Mode: Regulates the rotational force applied by the motor, ideal for applications requiring torque control.Allows adjustment of various control parameters to optimize performance according to the specific needs of each application. Industrial Applications Machinery automation, robotics, and production systems. Applications Wherever precise motion control is required. Precision Agriculture Motor control in tractors, harvesters, and dosing systems. Industrial Applications Machinery automation, robotics, and production systems. Accessibility Lifts, electric wheelchairs, and motorized assistance systems. Precision Agriculture Motor control in tractors, harvesters, and dosing systems. E-Mobility Applications in electric vehicles, e-bikes, and sustainable transport solutions. Accessibility Lifts, electric wheelchairs, and motorized assistance systems. E-Mobility Applications in electric vehicles, e-bikes, and sustainable transport solutions. Versatility and Efficiency Electronic motors offer a versatile and efficient solution for a wide range of applications, ensuring precision, reliability, and adaptability in industrial and mobility environments.
Contact Us

post.title: Wiper Systems Electronics
post.content:
ELECTRONICS
Discover Wiper Systems Electronics
Precise control of system movement Smooth and precise operation for improved visibility. Electronic Motors for Windshield Wiper Applications The electronic motors used in modern wiper system's movement offer advanced integration with the vehicle and enable precise control of system movement. These motors provide multiple advantages in terms of performance, efficiency, and advanced functionality. Advanced integration with the vehicle Optimal synchronization with the vehicle's systems. Precise control of system movement Smooth and precise operation for improved visibility. Advanced integration with the vehicle Optimal synchronization with the vehicle's systems. Features and Benefits
Vehicle integration via CAN or LIN communication
The electronic motors used in modern wiper systems offer advanced integration with the vehicle and enable precise control of system movement. These motors provide multiple advantages in terms of performance, energy efficiency, and advanced functionality.
Precise Speed and Wiping Angle Control
Thanks to their integrated electronics, these motors allow precise control of wiper speed and sweep angle, ensuring optimal performance under different weather conditions.
Advanced Additional Features
Active Parking: Allows the wiper blades to retract beneath the hood when not in use, enhancing the vehicle’s aerodynamics and overall aesthetics.Motor Synchronization: Synchronizes multiple motors for smooth, coordinated operation in complex systems.Washer Pump Integration: Facilitates combined control of the wiper motor and washer pump for enhanced cleaning efficiency.
Sweep angles greater than 180 degrees
These motors allow for wide sweep configurations, ensuring maximum windshield coverage to improve driver visibility.
Application in various transmission systems
They are compatible with both mechanical transmission systems (where the motor drives a linkage mechanism) and direct drive systems (direct drive on the wiper arm without the need for a mechanical transmission).
Oscillating or rotary motion control
Depending on the application, electronic motors can be configured to generate an oscillating motion (typical of windshield wipers) or a rotary motion (for applications with special cleaning systems).
The electronic motors used in modern wiper systems offer advanced integration with the vehicle and enable precise control of system movement. These motors provide multiple advantages in terms of performance, energy efficiency, and advanced functionality.Thanks to their integrated electronics, these motors allow precise control of wiper speed and sweep angle, ensuring optimal performance under different weather conditions.Active Parking: Allows the wiper blades to retract beneath the hood when not in use, enhancing the vehicle’s aerodynamics and overall aesthetics.Motor Synchronization: Synchronizes multiple motors for smooth, coordinated operation in complex systems.Washer Pump Integration: Facilitates combined control of the wiper motor and washer pump for enhanced cleaning efficiency.These motors allow for wide sweep configurations, ensuring maximum windshield coverage to improve driver visibility.They are compatible with both mechanical transmission systems (where the motor drives a linkage mechanism) and direct drive systems (direct drive on the wiper arm without the need for a mechanical transmission).Depending on the application, electronic motors can be configured to generate an oscillating motion (typical of windshield wipers) or a rotary motion (for applications with special cleaning systems). Smart Solutions in the industry Electronic wiper motors represent a key advancement in the automotive sector, offering greater flexibility, efficiency, and customization in system control.Their integration into the vehicle’s network, along with advanced functionalities, makes them an ideal solution for improving driver safety and comfort.
See Wiper System

post.title: Mechatronics
post.content:
DRIVE SYSTEMS At DOGA, we specialize in DC motors, and our Technical Center boasts advanced technological development capabilities along with a highly qualified team of engineers. This enables us to design customized and comprehensive solutions. Integrated mechatronic solutions Mechatronics is a core part of our work and we develop systems that integrate mechanics with electronics, achieving maximum control and precision. This combination allows us to offer specific, tailored solutions for each application. The result is fully integrated systems that ensure perfectly suited functionality to meet the needs.
Transform Your Ideas into Intelligent Systems Our extensive experience allows us to provide comprehensive solutions that often involve unique challenges. By leveraging mechatronics, we maximize outcomes, enhancing both functionality and efficiency.
Request Information
DRIVE SYSTEM ON A PICK-UP COVER Drive System to actuate on a pick-up (tonneau) cover. The Drive System includes compact gearmotor, electronic control unit and transmissions. Activated though our remote control the motor drives the cover to open/close. Our system is automatically calibrated, including anti-pinch and overload protection.
Get your Custom Solution

post.title: Electronics
post.content:
Electronics Always by your side, with an advanced organizational strategy designed for flexibility, ensuring a fully personalized experience tailored to your needs.
Discover Electronics
Discover Laboratory
Experts in Systems Wiper Systems, Drive Systems, and Tank Systems personalized experience tailored to your needs Specialists In Wiper Systems, Drive Systems, Tank Systems And Other Systems Components. Innovation & Software Integration with CANOpen, CAN J1939, and LIN Experts in Systems Wiper Systems, Drive Systems, and Tank Systems Electronic Design & Development Controllers for DC and BLDC motors Regulations & Validation Compliance with ISO, UNE-EN, EMC, and mechanical testing Innovation & Software Integration with CANOpen, CAN J1939, and LIN Electronic Design & Development Controllers for DC and BLDC motors Regulations & Validation Compliance with ISO, UNE-EN, EMC, and mechanical testing Electronics Development Team The electronics development team is made up of highly qualified engineers from various specialties. 01 Hardware Capabilities and Resources The hardware engineering team is responsible for designing controllers for DC and BLDC motors, complying with the strictest international standards and any specific customer requirements. They use Altium for hardware design and rely on P-Spice models for calculations and simulations.
02 Firmware Capabilities and Resources The firmware engineering team implements algorithms for DC and BLDC motors using advanced control techniques such as PID and FOC. Firmware is developed in C and assembly language, with automated processes in place for static code analysis, unit testing, and integration testing to ensure high quality.
03 Technology Our controllers feature 32-bit technology from manufacturers such as NXP and Texas Instruments.
04 Workflow In our pursuit of product excellence, we follow a CI/CD development workflow that enhances productivity and ensures quality throughout the different stages of firmware development.
01 Hardware Capabilities and Resources The hardware engineering team is responsible for designing controllers for DC and BLDC motors, complying with the strictest international standards and any specific customer requirements. They use Altium for hardware design and rely on P-Spice models for calculations and simulations. 02 Firmware Capabilities and Resources The firmware engineering team implements algorithms for DC and BLDC motors using advanced control techniques such as PID and FOC. Firmware is developed in C and assembly language, with automated processes in place for static code analysis, unit testing, and integration testing to ensure high quality. 03 Technology Our controllers feature 32-bit technology from manufacturers such as NXP and Texas Instruments. 04 Workflow In our pursuit of product excellence, we follow a CI/CD development workflow that enhances productivity and ensures quality throughout the different stages of firmware development. Services We provide tailored electronic solutions, ensuring seamless system integration and compliance with industry standards.
Electronic product integration
We help customers integrate our product and develop any type of electronics adapted to their system to achieve an optimal end-to-end solution.
Custom software development
We also develop custom software to meet our customers’ most demanding requirements.
Co-development and regulatory compliance
We carry out co-developments with our clients to help them comply with regulations, such as Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC), Electrical standards, or mechanical and climatic testing requirements.
We help customers integrate our product and develop any type of electronics adapted to their system to achieve an optimal end-to-end solution. We also develop custom software to meet our customers' most demanding requirements. We carry out co-developments with our clients to help them comply with regulations, such as Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC), Electrical standards, or mechanical and climatic testing requirements. Discover our lines Drive Systems Electronics
Wiper Systems Electronics
Testing And Validation Laboratory DOGA’s Laboratory is the department responsible for carrying out validation and research testing of our products, ensuring that each one meets the highest quality standards. We specialize in electric and electronic motors, control units, mechanical actuators, and wiper systems.https://vimeo.com/1106017479 Regulatory Standards at DOGA Our laboratory follows a standardized working methodology based on international standards and our customers’ specific requirements, ensuring the reliability and safety of our products. ISO(Customer-specific standards, adapted to their particular needs.) UNE-EN(European Rules)Customer-specific standards, tailored to their particular requirements.[mission_value_cards] Testing Laboratory Capabilities We have advanced equipment and state-of-the-art technology to carry out a wide range of tests covering different areas of product development and manufacturing.
Get in Touch
Testing And Validation Laboratory To certify the quality and compliance of our products with international regulations, we also collaborate with official bodies and accredited laboratories. Advanced Methodology for Electronic Product Validation In the validation and testing area for electronic products, we apply advanced testing methodologies tailored to the most demanding communication and functional standards. We develop custom test environments based on the specific requirements of our products and our customers. This allows us to ensure that each device undergoes detailed and effective testing under controlled conditions.To achieve this, we use state-of-the-art tools and software platforms that enable complex and precise testing. Our working methodology not only guarantees product reliability but also promotes continuous improvement and adds value to both DOGA and our customers.https://vimeo.com/1106018431 Commitment to quality and innovation At DOGA, we are committed to delivering products of the highest quality, using cutting-edge technology and advanced validation and testing methodologies. Thanks to our infrastructure, expertise, and collaboration with external laboratories, we ensure that every product meets the most stringent international standards, providing innovative technological solutions that benefit both our company and our customers.
DOGA Quality
Software, Tools, and Support Vector © We work with widely recognized automotive software tools such as Vector©, and with standard communication protocols – CANOpen, CAN J1939, and LIN – to facilitate the integration of electronic motors into the customer's system. Ectis We also offer the possibility of developing custom desktop software for integrating the electronic motor into the client’s application. This enables motor parameter configuration, access to diagnostics, and direct motor control.An example of this is our ECTIS application, which allows advanced configuration, monitoring, and control of DC and BLDC motors. It uses artificial intelligence to automatically optimize motor parameters based on performance and operating conditions, enabling faster and more precise adjustments.
Contact Us
Flexible parameter configuration
Adjust voltage, current, speed, acceleration, PID, and other key parameters.
Real-time monitoring
View interactive charts of speed, torque, temperature, and energy consumption.
Local and remote control
Send commands from a PC, mobile app, or cloud platform.
Communication protocols
Supports CAN, LIN, Modbus, Bluetooth, and Wi-Fi.
Data logging and predictive analysis
Store performance history and predict failures using AI.
AI-based parameter auto-tuning
Machine learning algorithms dynamically optimize values to improve efficiency and reduce wear.
Alerts and predictive maintenance
Receive notifications about overloads, overheating, and potential failures before they occur.
Adjust voltage, current, speed, acceleration, PID, and other key parameters.View interactive charts of speed, torque, temperature, and energy consumption.Send commands from a PC, mobile app, or cloud platform.Supports CAN, LIN, Modbus, Bluetooth, and Wi-Fi.Store performance history and predict failures using AI.Machine learning algorithms dynamically optimize values to improve efficiency and reduce wear.Receive notifications about overloads, overheating, and potential failures before they occur. meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_0_mvc_des: We perform tests on the electromechanical properties of motors, ensuring their performance complies with international standards.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_1_mvc_des: We carry out thorough testing to verify the electrical characteristics of products, ensuring safe and efficient operation under various working conditions.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_2_mvc_des: We evaluate the reliability and safety of the software in our control units and other electronic products, ensuring their integrity and long-term performance.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_3_mvc_des: We conduct tests to ensure our products do not generate electromagnetic interference and are resistant to external disturbances.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_4_mvc_des: We simulate extreme conditions of temperature, humidity, and other environmental factors to assess our products’ resistance to corrosion and wear.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_5_mvc_des: We perform tests that simulate real-use conditions to verify the products’ resistance to mechanical stress and vibrations.

meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_7_mvc_des: We carry out tests in accordance with international standards, such as those established by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE), to evaluate product durability throughout its lifecycle.

post.title: Drive Systems Division
post.content:
DRIVE SYSTEMS At DOGA, we have been at the forefront of manufacturing direct current (DC) motors for over 60 years. Our extensive product range is meticulously designed to meet the needs of any application requiring DC motors.
Discover Products
When Performance Meets Precision We offer motors with power outputs ranging from 2.5 to 1.500 W, available in 12, 24, 72, and up to 110 V. Our worm gear motors feature ratios from 12:1 to 81:1, while our planetary gear motors offer ratios from 4 to 308:1. Additionally, we provide electronic motors with custom-made interfaces, tailored to each client’s specific requirements. PRODUCTS Motor Designs that Fit You Perfectly Our team of engineers excels in designing unique and precise excels in designing unique and precise motors that can be complemented with gearboxes, brakes, encoders, and custom-programmed electronics. This electronics can be integrated or external, always aiming to offer infinite possibilities that make each application unique and special.
Discover Products
SOLUTIONS Our Mechatronic Solution At DOGA, we specialize in DC motors, and our Technical Center boasts advanced technological development capabilities along with a highly qualified team of engineers. This enables us to design customized and comprehensive solutions.
Discover Mechatronics

post.title: Privacy Policy
post.content: In compliance with the provisions of Organic Law 15/99, of December 13, on the Protection of Personal Data, and Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council, of April 27, 2016 (hereinafter, RGPD), we inform you that the personal information communicated will be incorporated into the registry of processing activities owned by DOGA GESTIÓ, SLU (hereinafter, DOGA) with CIF: B64834708, postal address: Calle Salvador Seguí, 5 - 08630 - Abrera, Barcelona - Spain, telephone +34 937704600 and email: info@doga.es. For any matter related to the processing of personal data by DOGA GESTIÓ, you can contact our Data Protection Officer at the following email address: dpd@doga.es.DOGA will collect the following personal data:EMPLOYMENT or CV form: Access form for the services of the DOGA platform dedicated to managing Human Resources selection processes and under the name EPRESELECCION.Data collected: professional area, name, surnames, telephone, e-mail, CV, cover letter.Purpose: management of the User’s candidacy for the selection processes published or other vacancies according to their professional profile that may arise within the DOGA GROUP.Storage periods: the data will be stored until you are awarded a job or until you request erasure of the same. They will therefore be kept for the time necessary for the purpose of the processing, and once completed, the public administration, judges and courts, will be available for a period of six years for the attention and defense of possible responsibilities arising from the processing.Legitimation: provision of the consent of the User.Registration and Monitoring Social Networks Corporate Accounts:Personal data that facilitates or enables social networks to become a follower of them. The corporate accounts of the social networks of DOGA are the following:TWITTER: https://twitter.com/DOGA_IntLINKEDIN: https://es.linkedin.com/company/dogaYOUTUBE: https://www.youtube.com/user/DOGAautomotiveINSTAGRAM: https://www.instagram.com/dogasport/Data collected: First name, NIF, contact address, address, email, contact telephone number.Purpose: monitoring social network.Legitimation: provision of the consent of the User.Storage periods: the data will be stored for the time necessary for the purpose of the processing, and once completed, the public administration, judges and courts, will be available for a period of six years for the attention and defense of possible responsibilities arising from the processing.Recipients:to the extent that it is necessary for each of the purposes expressed in the previous sections, your data will be transferred to the companies of the DOGA GROUP (DOGA GESTIÓ SLU, DOGA SA, DOGA ESPARREGUERA SL, DOGA PARTS SL, DOGA GRUP EMPRESARIAL SL and NAVINA SL) the relationship and identification of the Group Companies can be found on the home page of this Website (www.doga.es).The transfer or communication of data to third parties is not envisaged, except for the provision of third-party services as Data Processors with which DOGA has signed the corresponding Data Processors contracts and verified that they meet the sufficient guarantees to apply appropriate technical and organizational measures, in such a way that the processing is carried out in accordance with the requirements of the RGPD guaranteeing the protection of the rights of the interested party.The User has recognized their rights to access, rectification, erasure, limitation, opposition and portability of their data. In the same way, it is protected by the right to withdraw its consent for processing by DOGA. Likewise, he has the right to claim before the control authority.At any time, the User may exercise their rights by sending a communication indicating the right they wish to exercise, to DOGA at the address indicated above or by sending an email to the address rgpd@doga.es.DOGA has adopted the necessary technical and organizational measures to guarantee the security and integrity of the data, as well as to avoid its alteration, loss, processing or unauthorized access.The User expressly accepts the content of the legal notice that regulates the provision of services.The data requested in the different forms of the Website are strictly necessary to meet your request, which the User voluntarily communicates. The refusal to provide the data classified as obligatory will suppose the non-provision or the impossibility of accessing the service for which they were requested. Likewise, data may be provided voluntarily with the objective that the services can be provided in an optimal way.Likewise, in those cases in which third party data are provided, the User undertakes to inform them of the content of this privacy policy.The User agrees to communicate as soon as possible to DOGA any modification and rectification of their personal data to the effect that the information contained in the Activity Logs of DOGA are at all times updated.Based on the legitimate interest of DOGA the personal data of the User may be used to send electronic commercial communications about information of interest to DOGA, being able at any time to oppose such processing for commercial purposes by sending an email to the address rgpd@doga.es.© «DOGA GESTIÓ», SLU. 2018All rights reserved.DOWNLOAD ITALIAN VERSIONDOWNLOAD ENGLISH VERSION

Wherever you are in the world, we provide our expertise with a truly global reach, ensuring seamless support and tailored solutions to meet your unique needs.
We Are Where You Are

post.title: R&D & Quality
post.content:
DOGA is a multi-product company and has therefore defined a separate mission for each of its product lines.
Discover DOGA
Research, Development, & Innovation Research Extensive KNOW-HOW 1 Design Specialized ENGINEERING TEAM 2 Innovation From MECHANIC to ELECTRONIC 3 Developement Large and fully eqquiped TECHNICAL CENTER 4 https://vimeo.com/1106013391 Resources We have a team of over 70 people and a 2,300 m² technical center, providing local engineering support alongside technology partners. We specialize in product engineering, software and hardware development, simulation, prototyping, and testing in specialized laboratories. TEAM OF MORE THAN 70 PEOPLE 2.300 sq m TECHNICAL CENTER LOCAL ENGINEERING SUPPORT TECHNOLOGICAL PARTNERS PRODUCT ENGINEERING SOFTWARE AND HARDWARESIMULATION SIMULATION PROTOTYPING LABORATORIES Laboratory capabilities 10 Standards & Certifications ISOUNE-ENCustomer Standards 01 Motor Performance Motor performance test benchesMotor endurance test benches 02 Complete Systems Performance Programmable endurance test systems 03 Electrical Tests Electrical tests 04 Software Test & Validation Pruebas y validación de software 05 EMC Testing Radiated Emissions TestsImmunity TestsESD (Electrostatic Discharge) Tests 06 Mechanical & Vibration Tests Vibration Test BenchesUniversal Test Benches (Extracción, Compresión y Torsión) 07 Climatic Tests Climatic ChambersHumidistatic ChamberCorrosion TestsSalt Spray ChamberChemical Tests 08 IP Tests IP Test Bench (IPX4 – IPX9K) 09 Noise Analysis Noise analysis
10 Standards & Certifications ISOUNE-ENCustomer Standards 01 Motor Performance Motor performance test benchesMotor endurance test benches 02 Complete Systems Performance Programmable endurance test systems 03 Electrical Tests Electrical tests 04 Software Test & Validation Pruebas y validación de software 05 EMC Testing Radiated Emissions TestsImmunity TestsESD (Electrostatic Discharge) Tests 06 Mechanical & Vibration Tests Vibration Test BenchesUniversal Test Benches (Extracción, Compresión y Torsión) 07 Climatic Tests Climatic ChambersHumidistatic ChamberCorrosion TestsSalt Spray ChamberChemical Tests 08 IP Tests IP Test Bench (IPX4 – IPX9K) 09 Noise Analysis Noise analysis Built on Quality, Driven by Innovation At DOGA, quality is more than just a standard – it’s a commitment. For over 60 years, we have been dedicated to delivering high-performance solutions that exceed industry expectations. Our quality assurance processes ensure that every product leaving our facilities meets the highest standards of performance.
Our Approach to Quality
Advanced Manufacturing: Combining cutting-edge technology with experienced craftsmanship, we ensure every product is manufactured to perfection.Certifications: DOGA is certified under IATF 16949 and ISO 14001, ensuring compliance with global quality standards.Rigorous Testing: All products undergo thorough testing for performance, safety, and durability in the most demanding conditions.Continuous Improvement: Our teams are committed to innovation and ongoing improvement, ensuring we stay ahead of the curve in quality and efficiency.
Customer-Centric Quality
Our focus on quality extends beyond production – it defines our relationship with our customers. We work closely with you to understand your needs, customize solutions, and deliver results that add real value to your business
Quality Policy and Certifications
Our quality policy and certifications underline our commitment to quality and global best practices. DOGA holds the following certifications, reflecting our dedication to excellence.
Why choose DOGA
Proven track record in manufacturing and innovation.Products trusted by global leaders in automotive, industrial, and other sectors.An unwavering focus on delivering value through quality and reliability.When you choose DOGA, you choose a partner that prioritizes quality in every step, ensuring your projects succeed without compromise.

post.title: About us
post.content:
Productos
Discover DOGA
Strategy, Values & Knowhow At DOGA, we strive to be competitive, to specialize, to expand our resources, and above all, to ensure that our most valued knowledge and core values can be put at the service of our clients by offering a project that meets the highest expectations.We grow in an international environment, and both new markets and the cultural diversity in which we operate provide us with an extraordinary source of knowledge and fresh perspectives, allowing us to naturally adapt to future globally-oriented projects.Enthusiasm and perseverance are our hallmark — and for that reason alone, we can say that we don't have a single defined goal, because our possibilities are endless. Mission & Values [mission_value_cards] DOGA Mission DOGA is a multi-product company and has therefore defined a separate mission for each of its product lines.
Contact us
DOGA Core Values DOGA core values define how the company and its staff must act.
Leadership Demonstrate leadership within a team, motivating and delegating responsibilities to achieve common goals with autonomy and efficiency.
Communication The capacity to express clearly and understandably one’s own opinions and ideas. The ability to convey one’s own viewpoints precisely and intelligibly.
Resolution and Decision Making
The capacity to express clearly and understandably one’s own opinions and ideas. The ability to convey one’s own viewpoints precisely and intelligibly.
Creativity / Innovation Seeking imaginative solutions to problems related with work and being able to find innovative alternatives others than those normally used for resolution. Includes de capacity to bring new ideas that can turn into improvements or introduce changes.
Teamwork Actively contribute to the achievement of a common goal. Collaborate in the establishment of friendly relationships within the group and the creation of a demanding but loose working spirit.
Negotiation Be skilful when reaching satisfactory agreements that are based on a ‘win-win’ philosophy. Be able to show flexibility with new viewpoints when trying to reach an agreement.
Adaptability / Flexibility Ability to adjust effectively to different environments, people, and situations by understanding diverse perspectives and adapting to changes in work methods and technology.
Proactivity Be capable of identifying problems, obstacles or opportunities, both current and future and respond to these. This carries along with it the capacity to take on one’s own work from a broad and non-restrictive angle, by understanding that one’s role may mean undertaking projects, activities or tasks that are different from one’s job description.
Achievement orientation The capacity to steer one’s own activity toward goal achievement. It is displayed when there is a strong interest to perform professionally beyond the standard. Standards may make reference to improving one’s own past performance, exceeding others’ performance or achieving challenging or even new goals.
Customer orientation Demonstrate sensibility toward the needs or requests from existing, potential or internal customers. Focus the efforts to discover and satisfy the needs of customers in order to satisfactorily service them.

meta.a_mission_: Ser líder mundial en sistemas de limpiaparabrisas y lavaparabrisas para series pequeñas y medianas.
Apoyar a los clientes con soluciones innovadoras de motores de CC y motorreductores de hasta 48 voltios.
Ser líder mundial en soluciones innovadoras para tanques de plástico y otras piezas de plástico moldeadas por soplado, para series de volumen pequeño y mediano.
Proporcionar soluciones personalizadas para elevalunas para series de pequeño y mediano volumen.
Aumentar nuestro valor añadido a nuestros clientes ofreciendo productos complementarios: sistemas de aire, resortes de gas y espejos.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_0_mvc_des: To be the world leader in wiper and washer systems for small and medium production series.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_1_mvc_des: Supporting customers with innovative DC motor and gear motor solutions up to 48 volts.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_2_mvc_des: To be the world leader in innovative solutions for plastic tanks and other blow-molded plastic parts for small and medium volume series.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_3_mvc_des: Providing customized window regulator solutions for small and medium volume series.
meta.a_mission_n_value_card_details_4_mvc_des: Increasing our added value to customers by offering complementary products: air systems, gas springs, and mirrors.

post.title: Sustainability
post.content:
Productos
Committed to a sustainable future
Know more information
The 3 Pillars of Sustainability In 1987, the UN Brundtland Commission defined sustainability as "meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs".The Triple Bottom Line, or 3P, is a way to define a company's sustainability through three elements: economic (Profit), environmental (Planet), and social (People). Environment, Social, Governance From this focus on these elements, the acronym ESG (Environment, Social, Governance) emerged. Enviromental ESTRATEGIC GOALS and DEVELOPMENT PROJECTS Improve the systematic to reduce the environmental impact of the operations of the company contributing to the conservation of its environment.Improve environmentalperformance at DOGA Group level Comprehensive energy transition and climate change mitigation plan Social ESTRATEGIC GOALS and DEVELOPMENT PROJECTS Promote the retention and development of DOGA talent Implement an eective strategy for improving equality and inclusionEnsure that manufactured products meet the highest standards of excellence and meet customers' needs, including their expectations for sustainability
Ensuring a safe and healthy working environment Ensure a safe and healthy working environment by implementing policies that prevent accidents at work and promote practices that contribute to the health and welfare of workers. Governance ESTRATEGIC GOALS and DEVELOPMENT PROJECTS Improve DOGA Group’s position on Sustainability Strengthen the perception and reputation of the company in terms of its commitment and actions related to environmental, social and economic sustainability. Develop a risk management and criminal compliance system at DOGA Identify, assess and mitigate the legal and compliance risks faced by the Group in all its operations and business units, ensuring the integrity of business operations and protecting the reputation and interest of the Group against possible legal sanctions, nancial damage or loss of condence by stakeholders. Traction of the value chain on ESG topics Improve the systematic to reduce the environmental impact of the operations of the company contributing to the conservation of its environment Improve product quality and customer satisfaction Ensure that manufactured products meet the highest standards of excellence and meet customers' needs, including their expectations for sustainability. SUSTAINABILITY COMMITTEE In DOGA there is a Sustainablility Committee, a cross-functional and equitable team composed ofmembers from dierent areas: Environment, Purchasing, Human Resources, Commercial, Health andSafety, Product Engineering, Subsidiaries, and Sustainability.From this committee, we work to achieve its main objectives, and at DOGA we are developing actions to integrate the 17 SDGs into our daily operations:Implement sustainable projects and initiatives within the company, primarily those outlined in the 2024-2027 Sustainability Master Plan.Support the achievement of Sustainability-related indicators (Environmental, Social, and Governance).Participate in events promoted by the Sustainability Committee.Suggest improvements and contribute ideas in this area. Developed for sustainability Precision Farming Enhancing agricultural efficiency with cutting-edge motorized solutions for precise planting and harvesting.
See Product
Solar Tracker Maximizing solar energy output with intelligent tracking systems that follow the sun's movement.
See Product
Mobility E-Bike Motor Powering the future of urban mobility with high-performance electric bike motors for a seamless ride.

Commitment to Sustainability & Transparency Discover our initiatives and reports that reflect our dedication to environmental responsibility, social impact, and corporate governance. EINF Report
